mnemonics.title: TLS Handshake Steps
mnemonics.content: CHILL-CAFE: Client Hello → Server Hello → Identity (Certificate) → Key Exchange → Let's Cipher (Change Cipher Spec) → Application Data Flow → Finalize (Finished)

mnemonics.title: Record Protocol Functions
mnemonics.content: EAC: Encrypt → Authenticate → (optional Compress) - Remember 'Eat All Carrots' with Compression being optional

mnemonics.title: Cipher Suite Components
mnemonics.content: KABH: Key Exchange → Authentication → Bulk Cipher → Hash Function - 'Kings Attack Big Halls'

mnemonics.title: TLS 1.3 Improvements
mnemonics.content: ZEST: Zero-RTT → Ephemeral Keys Only → Simplified Handshake → TLS 1.2 Support Removed

acronyms.usage: Core security method in TLS 1.3 combining encryption and authentication
Allows hosting multiple SSL certificates on one IP address
Ensures session keys aren't compromised even if long-term keys are

visualTips.visualization: Imagine building a secure bridge: 1) Exchange blueprints (Hello messages) 2) Verify construction permits (certificates) 3) Install lockboxes (key exchange) 4) Seal bridge surface (Change Cipher Spec)
Visualize a digital passport with holograms (digital signatures), personal data (public key), and issuing authority stamp (CA signature)
Think of a VIP concert wristband - once verified, you get faster entry (resumed session) without full security check
Picture sealed armored envelopes containing fragmented messages, each with tamper-proof tape (MAC) and lock (encryption)

analogies.analogy: Like diplomatic protocol: 1) Exchange credentials 2) Verify identities using trusted documents 3) Agree on encrypted communication method 4) Seal meeting minutes in diplomatic pouches
Acts like a passport office - issues trusted IDs (certificates) after verification, recognized globally through trust chains
Like two spies meeting: 'I can speak French, German, or Morse code' (client ciphers) → 'Let's use German with one-time pads' (server selection)
